I've been led to believe in various locations and discussions on the internet that, in general, Europeans have a lot less leeway in textbook selection and developing their own curriculum than Americans/people in the U.S. If this is in fact true—which I believe it is—what do you think about them including something in the book which makes it virtually unusable for the U.S. market, which is slowly shifting towards a comprehensible or reading based approach (And this is a shift that can happen more readily here because teachers largely don't need approval)? Did they just not care?
